title: Bilateral monopoly revisited: price formation, efficiency and countervailing powers
body: "This discussion paper is the outcome of Professor Flavio Toxvaerd’s UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) Policy Fellowship at the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA). During his 18 months at the CMA, Professor Toxvaerd studied the role of input market power in shaping prices, quantities and consumer outcomes.  The paper nests several common bargaining protocols in a single bilateral monopoly model, allowing policymakers to better understand how changes in input market power affect consumer outcomes.   Apply for the next UKRI Policy Fellowship at the CMA.  "
